---
title: "Accommodations"
subtitle: "Hotels by City"
date: "2026-06-04"
categories: [logistics, hotels, planning]
toc: true
---

## Overview

Accommodations for 16 nights across Italy (Pisa, Florence, Naples, Puglia).

**Booking Strategy:**
- Book 2-3 months ahead for best selection
- Prioritize location over luxury
- Check cancellation policies
- Read recent reviews

---

## Pisa (1 Night: June 4-5)

### Options

**If Overnighting in Pisa:**

**Location Priority:** Near Pisa Centrale station (easy train to Florence next morning)

**Mid-Range Options:**
- **Hotel Bologna** - Near station, classic Italian hotel
- **Royal Victoria** - Along Arno river, historic property
- **Hotel Pisa Tower** - Modern, near tower (but touristy area)

**Budget Options:**
- Airbnb near station
- Hotel di Stefano

**Luxury:**
- Grand Hotel Duomo - Near Leaning Tower

### Booking Considerations

- **Early morning departure:** Choose hotel near station
- **See the tower:** Stay near Piazza dei Miracoli (but pricier)
- **Authentic Pisa:** Along Arno away from tower

---

## Florence (4 Nights: June 6–10)

### Confirmed Booking — Aurora apartments Piazzetta

**Aurora apartments Piazzetta**

| Detail | Info |
|--------|------|
| **Address** | Via Por Santa Maria, 8, Florence, Tuscany 50122 |
| **Check-in** | Saturday June 6, 4:00 PM |
| **Check-out** | Wednesday June 10, **10:00 AM sharp** |
| **Guests** | Dan Green, Jane Mooney |
| **Confirmation** | HMMKBKP8JY |
| **Platform** | Airbnb |
| **Host** | Lorenzo: +39 055 093 0093 |

**Location:** Via Por Santa Maria runs directly from Piazza della Signoria down to Ponte Vecchio — one of the most central streets in Florence. The Uffizi is steps away; the Duomo is a 10-minute walk north.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Checkout Is 10:00 AM — Hard

Checkout is 10:00 AM sharp. The Italo train to Naples departs Florence at 12:28 PM — pack the night before (Day 5 evening) and plan to leave the apartment by 10:00 AM with bags. That gives roughly 2.5 hours before the train.
:::

---

## Naples (3 Nights: June 10–13)

### Confirmed Booking — Casa Wenner Large

**Casa Wenner Large — Naples Center Chiaia Plebiscito**

| Detail | Info |
|--------|------|
| **Address** | Via Monte di Dio, 66, Naples 80132 |
| **Check-in** | Wednesday June 10, 3:00 PM |
| **Check-out** | Saturday June 13, **10:00 AM sharp** |
| **Guests** | Daniel Green, Jane Mooney |
| **Confirmation** | HMTBH3HCCX |
| **Platform** | Airbnb |
| **Cost** | $826.57 |

**Host contacts:**
- Pierfrancesco: +39 335 607 1111
- Antonella: +39 320 118 6998

**Location:** Chiaia neighborhood — upscale, safe, excellent restaurants, 10-minute walk to the historic center and Spaccanapoli. Via Monte di Dio is a quiet street running up from the waterfront toward the hill.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Checkout Is 10:00 AM — Hard

The Airbnb checkout is 10:00 AM with no flexibility. Pack the night before. On Day 9 (June 13), your flight to Brindisi isn't until 3:05 PM — the airport taxi departs around 1:30 PM — so you'll have 3+ hours with your bags after checkout. Ask about luggage storage or find a café nearby.
:::

---

## Puglia (6 Nights: June 13-19)

### Status: Pending Tour Confirmation

Puglia accommodations will likely be **included in the organized tour package** (June 14-19).

### Potential Scenarios

**Scenario A: Tour Includes All Accommodations**
- Most likely
- Hotels selected by tour operator
- May rotate between towns or stay in one base
- Typically 3-4 star properties

**Scenario B: We Select Own Accommodations**
- Less likely if organized tour
- Would coordinate with tour meeting points

### Brindisi (1 Night: June 13–14)

**Confirmed Booking — Hotel Executive Inn**

| Detail | Info |
|--------|------|
| **Hotel** | Hotel Executive Inn |
| **Address** | Via Pozzo Traiano, 24, Brindisi 72100 |
| **Check-in** | Saturday June 13, 2:00 PM |
| **Check-out** | Sunday June 14 |
| **Room** | 1 Deluxe Double Room, 2 adults |
| **Platform** | Expedia |
| **Itinerary** | 73435335535420 |
| **Total cost** | €166.48 (room €145.89 + taxes €14.59 + fees €6.00) |

Arriving from Brindisi airport at 6:25 PM — taxi to hotel is ~€12–15, 10–15 minutes. Check-in will be evening arrival, well after 2 PM opening.

### Possible Tour Locations

If tour rotates locations, potential overnight towns:

**Alberobello:**
- Stay IN a trullo (unique experience)
- Hotel options or trulli rentals

**Lecce:**
- Centro storico (Baroque old town)
- Near Piazza del Duomo

**Polignano a Mare / Monopoli:**
- Coastal towns
- Sea view hotels

**Ostuni:**
- White city hilltop
- Charming B&Bs

### Masseria Stays

**What:** Traditional fortified farmhouses, now luxury hotels

**Experience:**
- Rural Puglia setting
- Often family-run
- Excellent regional cuisine
- Swimming pools
- Olive groves and vineyards

**Examples:**
- Masseria Torre Coccaro
- Masseria San Domenico
- Masseria Torre Maizza

**Note:** Premium masserie are €250-500/night, but tour may include mid-range options.

---

## General Booking Tips

### When to Book

- **2-3 months ahead:** Best selection and rates
- **Peak season (June-August):** Book earlier
- **Flexible dates:** Use booking.com to compare
- **Last minute:** Risky in summer

### What to Check

**Before Booking:**
- [ ] Recent reviews (last 6 months)
- [ ] Location on map (walkability)
- [ ] Cancellation policy
- [ ] Breakfast included?
- [ ] Air conditioning (essential in June)
- [ ] Elevator (if mobility concerns)
- [ ] WiFi quality (check reviews)
- [ ] Safe neighborhood (especially Naples)

**Confirmation:**
- [ ] Save confirmation email
- [ ] Screenshot booking details
- [ ] Note hotel phone number
- [ ] Add address to Google Maps
- [ ] Check check-in time (usually 2-3 PM)
- [ ] Note check-out time (usually 10-11 AM)

### Booking Sites

**Comparison:**
- [Booking.com](https://www.booking.com) - Best selection, easy cancellation
- [Hotels.com](https://www.hotels.com) - Loyalty rewards
- [Airbnb](https://www.airbnb.com) - Apartments, local character
- Hotel direct websites - Sometimes better rates, perks

**Tip:** Check multiple sites, then book direct with hotel if same price (better service if issues arise).

---

## Packing for Hotels

**Bring:**
- [ ] Power adapters (Italy uses Type C, F, L plugs)
- [ ] Small flashlight (power outages happen)
- [ ] Earplugs (especially Naples, thin walls)
- [ ] Portable phone charger
- [ ] Travel-size toiletries (many hotels have minimal amenities)

**Hotel Amenities Vary:**
- Hairdryer: Usually provided
- Shampoo/soap: Often basic or not provided
- Towels: Provided (but sometimes thin)
- Slippers: Rarely provided
- Robes: Only luxury hotels

---

## Budget Planning

### Estimated Costs (Per Night, Double Occupancy)

| City | Budget | Mid-Range | Luxury |
|------|--------|-----------|--------|
| **Pisa** | €50-70 | €80-120 | €150+ |
| **Florence** | €60-100 | €100-200 | €250+ |
| **Naples** | €50-80 | €80-150 | €200+ |
| **Puglia** | TBD (tour) | TBD (tour) | €200+ |

### Total Trip Estimate

**Mid-Range Approach:**
- Pisa: 1 night × €100 = €100
- Florence: 4 nights × €150 = €600
- Naples: 2 nights × €120 = €240
- Puglia: 6 nights included in tour or ~€900
- **Total:** ~€1,840 (not including Puglia if tour-provided)

---

## Accommodation Checklist

### 3 Months Before Trip
- [ ] Finalize itinerary (dates, cities)
- [ ] Research neighborhoods
- [ ] Set budget
- [ ] Create shortlist of properties
- [ ] Check reviews

### 2 Months Before
- [ ] Book Florence hotels (highest priority)
- [ ] Book Naples hotels
- [ ] Book Pisa if overnighting
- [ ] Confirm Puglia tour accommodation status

### 1 Month Before
- [ ] Confirm all reservations
- [ ] Print confirmations
- [ ] Save addresses to Google Maps
- [ ] Note hotel phone numbers
- [ ] Check cancellation policies again

### Week Before Trip
- [ ] Email hotels to confirm arrival time
- [ ] Review check-in procedures
- [ ] Note any special instructions (key pickup, parking, etc.)

---

**Next Steps:** See [Restaurants](restaurants.qmd) for dining recommendations by city.
